response:
Sec. 1  
People who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to peanut proteins in the same way it would react to any other allergen  
Living underwater does not eliminate exposure to peanut proteins as these proteins can be present in food or other substances that enter the water  
Allergic reactions are triggered by the immune system not by the physical environment in which a person lives  

Sec. 2  
The inability to live underwater is not related to the allergy itself but rather to the practical challenges of living in an aquatic environment  
Peanut allergies do not affect a person's ability to survive in water unless there are other factors that make underwater living impossible  
Therefore the question is based on a misunderstanding of how allergies function and the conditions required for underwater living
